req.title: Hr-Admin views timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a happy-path where an authenticated hr-admin views a valid timesheet and an audit record is created (EP), (2) a forbidden-access path where a non-hr-admin role is denied with HTTP 403 (NEG/DT), (3) an unauthenticated access path returning HTTP 401 (NEG), (4) a boundary case where the timesheet ID is the minimum valid integer boundary value (BVA), and (5) a state-transition case verifying the UI form transitions from 'loading' to 'loaded' state once the timesheet is rendered (STC). Together these cases cover all stated expected outcomes and error pathways.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_views_valid_timesheet_positive
preconditions: A timesheet with a known valid ID exists in the system
actions: Assert timesheet entity ID is present in the response/page
Assert audit record is written for the view event
expected: Timesheet data is visible on the page
Audit record exists for the view action
trace: R-WEB-020: authenticated hr-admin can view timesheet
R-WEB-020: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_views_valid_timesheet_positive(
    hr_admin_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for hr-admin
    valid_timesheet_id,   # <fixture>: int ID of a seeded timesheet
    audit_log_client,     # <fixture>: client to query audit records
    base_url,             # <fixture>: str base URL of hr-app
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id}")

    expect(page).to_have_url(f"{base_url}/timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id}")
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-id']")).to_contain_text(
        str(valid_timesheet_id)
    )

    response_status = page.evaluate("() => window.__lastResponseStatus")
    assert response_status == 200, f"Expected 200, got {response_status}"

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(
        entity="timesheet",
        entity_id=valid_timesheet_id,
        action="view",
    )
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record not written for view action"
    assert audit_records[-1]["entity_id"] == str(valid_timesheet_id)

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_timesheet_negative
preconditions: A timesheet with a known valid ID exists
actions: Assert timesheet data is NOT rendered
expected: No audit record written for a forbidden attempt
trace: R-WEB-020: error pathway – 403 if role forbidden
R-WEB-020: only hr-admin may view timesheet
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_view_timesheet_negative(
    employee_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for employee role
    valid_timesheet_id,   # <fixture>: int ID of a seeded timesheet
    audit_log_client,     # <fixture>: client to query audit records
    base_url,             # <fixture>: str
):
    page = employee_page
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id}")

    error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='error-forbidden']"
                                 ", [data-testid='http-403']")
    expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()

    timesheet_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-id']")
    expect(timesheet_locator).not_to_be_visible()

    response_status = page.evaluate("() => window.__lastResponseStatus")
    assert response_status == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response_status}"

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(
        entity="timesheet",
        entity_id=valid_timesheet_id,
        action="view",
    )
    forbidden_records = [
        r for r in audit_records if r.get("outcome") == "forbidden"
    ]
    assert len(forbidden_records) == 0 or forbidden_records[-1]["outcome"] != "success"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_timesheet_negative
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
A timesheet with a known valid ID exists
actions: Attempt to navigate to /timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id} without authentication
Assert HTTP response is 401 or redirect to login
expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized returned OR redirect to login page
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_timesheet_negative(
    unauthenticated_page, # <fixture>: fresh Playwright Page with no session cookies
    valid_timesheet_id,   # <fixture>: int
    base_url,             # <fixture>: str
):
    page = unauthenticated_page
    response = page.goto(f"{base_url}/timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id}")

    final_url = page.url
    is_redirected_to_login = "login" in final_url or "signin" in final_url
    status_is_401 = (response is not None and response.status == 401)

    assert is_redirected_to_login or status_is_401, (
        f"Expected 401 or login redirect, got status={response.status if response else 'None'} "
        f"url={final_url}"
    )

    timesheet_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-id']")
    expect(timesheet_locator).not_to_be_visible()

tests.name: test_hr_admin_views_minimum_boundary_timesheet_id_boundary
preconditions: A timesheet seeded with the minimum valid ID (1) exists
actions: Navigate to /timesheets/1 (minimum boundary ID) as hr-admin
Assert timesheet with ID 1 is displayed
expected: Timesheet entity with ID 1 rendered on page
Audit record created for the view event
trace: R-WEB-020: boundary – minimum valid timesheet ID
R-WEB-020: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MIN_TIMESHEET_ID = 1  # boundary value: lowest valid integer ID

def test_hr_admin_views_minimum_boundary_timesheet_id_boundary(
    hr_admin_page,      # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for hr-admin
    seed_timesheet,     # <fixture>: callable(id) ensures timesheet with given ID exists
    audit_log_client,   # <fixture>
    base_url,           # <fixture>: str
):
    seed_timesheet(MIN_TIMESHEET_ID)
    page = hr_admin_page
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/timesheets/{MIN_TIMESHEET_ID}")

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-id']")).to_contain_text(
        str(MIN_TIMESHEET_ID)
    )

    response_status = page.evaluate("() => window.__lastResponseStatus")
    assert response_status == 200, f"Expected 200 for min ID, got {response_status}"

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(
        entity="timesheet",
        entity_id=MIN_TIMESHEET_ID,
        action="view",
    )
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "No audit record for min boundary timesheet view"

tests.name: test_timesheet_form_state_transitions_from_loading_to_loaded
actions: Assert form enters 'loading' state immediately after navigation starts
Assert form transitions to 'loaded' state with timesheet data visible
expected: Timesheet data visible after loaded state
No error state entered during transition
trace: R-WEB-020: UI form must display the timesheet (state transition: loading->loaded)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_timesheet_form_state_transitions_from_loading_to_loaded(
    hr_admin_page,       # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page for hr-admin
    valid_timesheet_id,  # <fixture>: int
    base_url,            # <fixture>: str
):
    page = hr_admin_page
    loading_states = []

    def capture_state(response):
        if f"/timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id}" in response.url:
            loading_states.append("network_response_received")

    page.on("response", capture_state)

    with page.expect_navigation(url=f"{base_url}/timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id}"):
        page.goto(f"{base_url}/timesheets/{valid_timesheet_id}")

    loading_indicator = page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-loading']")
    loaded_indicator  = page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-id']")
    error_indicator   = page.locator("[data-testid='timesheet-error']")

    expect(loaded_indicator).to_be_visible(timeout=10_000)
    expect(error_indicator).not_to_be_visible()

    assert "network_response_received" in loading_states, \
        "Navigation did not trigger a network response (loading state not entered)"
